what_it_does: A running operational log for the guided-review autonomous cron loop: each fire re-reads it to re-orient, records standing context, gates, in-flight/blocked/needs-Tim status, what was built/verified/held, coordination hazards, and next-fire actions.
observations: It is a continuity/state file meant to survive crashes and compaction.
It contains many timestamped fire entries for 2026-06-09 with build outcomes, blockers, and decisions.
It tracks dependencies on cognition's C seam, coherence gates, and cross-session claims.

It records a progression from a blocked cast assertion through landing the walkthrough cast, screen_reader role, FE show-me lane, B1 streaming, generate engine, generate follow-on, critical review, F2 store-mismatch fix, and V-A registry-generation workflow.
It documents coordination hazards such as concurrent-commit races, uncommitted cross-session edits, dead-builder recovery, and the commit-pathspec rule.
It distinguishes 'verified by use' from 'needs-tim' and refuses to green-paint or commit on red gates.
It ends with the V-A registry-generation commit+record role and RG8 blocked on registration/orphans.
It includes environment notes such as a chrome process leak, a setsid fix, and tldraw bleed fixes.
standouts: The file is a self-updating autonomous-loop state log that also serves as a decision audit trail.
It captures a multi-session build narrative ending in the F2 make-or-break loop verified end-to-end and the V-A registry-generation handoff.
It repeatedly surfaces verification honesty and refuses to commit on red gates or touch others' uncommitted work.
It documents concrete coordination hazards and recovery rules rather than hiding them.
conventions: Timestamped fire entries with status markers like HOLD, BLOCKED, ✅, and ⚠.
Cites commit SHAs, suite counts, and file:line references.
Distinguishes file-disjoint changes from shared-file changes.
Uses explicit pathspec commits to avoid concurrent-commit races.
Follows a 'not spinning' rule: do not re-scope or half-build.
Respects cross-session claims and never touches another session's uncommitted files.
Uses an honest verification taxonomy: verified by use, needs-tim, and unverified.
concerns: It is very long, append-only, and dense, so readability and compaction may degrade.
It relies heavily on external context such as commit SHAs, suite names, and other sessions without inline definitions.
Some entries contain self-corrections and corrections², which could confuse readers.
It ends mid-narrative with unresolved blocked items and pre-existing red suites.
It includes environment-specific operational details such as killing chrome processes.
notes: This is the guided-review autonomous loop's living flight recorder: context, gates, what got built, what is blocked, and the audit trail of decisions across cron fires.
questions: What are the exact contents of the Completion Criteria file referenced throughout?
What are the precise roles and current file claims of the cognition and coherence sessions?
What do the many suite names such as cast_beyond_listening_acceptance and wire_trigger_acceptance actually test?
What is the precise operator-approval flow for apply-mode and registry write-back?
What is the final physical state of the working tree at the end of the log?
apparent_themes: Autonomous but bounded: build only when gated green, hold otherwise.
Registry-is-truth and no fabrication of addresses.
Process hygiene such as explicit pathspecs and spawned-process cleanup.
intention_signals: It tries to keep a distributed autonomous build honest and current by forcing each cron fire to re-read state, report only real changes, avoid spinning, and escalate blockers to Tim rather than guess-build.
novelty: The file functions as both operational state and a narrative audit log for a multi-agent cron loop, with explicit anti-patterns such as no green-paint, no bare git commit, and no touching others' uncommitted work embedded as conventions.
contribution: A concrete example of how to maintain continuity, honesty, and coordination boundaries in an autonomous multi-session build loop; it keeps blockers visible and prevents unverified commits.
summary_for_embedding: A timestamped operational state log for the guided-review autonomous cron loop, recording context, gates, built/verified/hold items, cross-session coordination hazards, and next-fire actions across many fires.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a crash-robust re-orientation and audit trail for an autonomous build session so each cron fire knows what is done, blocked, and safe to do next.
